text:
Hunter × Hunter: The Stage is a modern play adaptation of the Hunter × Hunter series.

Information

Part 1

When Gon was a child, he was told by Kite, a young Hunter who happened to be visiting Whale Island. That my father, Ging, who I thought was dead, is alive. And we are told that he is a great Hunter. Eventually, at the age of 12, Gon decides to meet Ging in order to become the same hunter as Ging. Leaving his hometown and challenging the Hunter Exam...! Along with his friends Killua, Kurapika, Leorio, and other aspiring Hunters, Gon confronts the looming obstacles. Is Gon safe? Can you become a Hunter...!?

Part 2

"September 1st in Yorknew City!! ──" After completing the Hunter Exam, Gon, Killua, Kurapika, and Leorio have mastered Nen in their respective places. They are reunited in Yorknew City, where the world's largest auction is held. At the same time, Chrollo's Phantom Troupe has also converged in Yorknew to steal all the auction items. The Phantom Troupe, commonly known as the "Spiders", is a group of thieves who had taken the lives of all of Kurapika's brethren, the Kurta tribe. Fates intertwined like chains. Kurapika's eyes are stained scarlet with anger and sadness. Constraints and Commitments. Eventually, Kurapika takes revenge and confronts the Phantom Troupe alone. Gon and the others, will you be able to lead the hearts of your precious friends to peace?

Part 3

“Gon and Killua enter Greed Island!! ──” Stepping into the legendary game, they are welcomed by Eta and guided to summon their books. But danger awaits at every corner. Mysterious opponents, deadly bombs, and fierce battles test their courage and skill. Under Bisky’s strict training, Gon and Killua sharpen their Nen, facing challenges that push them to their limits. Razor’s fearsome dodgeball and encounters with Hisoka and the Phantom Troupe turn the game into a thrilling spectacle of power, strategy, and music. Can they survive the perils of Greed Island and overcome the treacherous Bomber? Only their courage, friendship, and determination will decide their fate.

Summary

Part 1

The Captain navigates his ship through the torrential waters and, from his crew members, learns that the death toll that year was the same. He spots a boy, who warns that an even greater storm is coming based on what the seagulls have told him. Reminded of someone he once knew from Whale Island, the Captain asks for the boy's name, who responds that it's Gon, and he wants to become a Hunter like his father.

A flashback of Gon talking to his Aunt Mito about his resolve to become a Hunter after learning his father was alive. Another flashback depicts how Gon learned that Ging was a Hunter by a chance encounter with his mentee Kite when he saved Gon from a Foxbear. Convincing Kite to spare the Foxbear cub, named it Kon, prompting Kite to remark on Ging's phrase that good Hunters are well-liked by animals.

Before departing for his adventure, Mito gives him his backpack and fishing rod, and Gon reveals he knew that Mito was lying to him about Ging being dead.

In the present, Gon warns the Captain that the next storm will be twice as bad as the previous one. With that in mind, the Captain demands the passengers who can't handle the oncoming storm to abandon the ship, and all but Gon and two others named Leorio and Kurapika stay behind. The Captain asks for their reasons for wanting to become Hunters, and all but Gon answer his question. At the same time, Kurapika and Leorio refuse, citing personal matters, and they get into a spat. Their refusal to answer the Captain's question made him demand they leave his ship as the Hunter Exam had already begun. Given one more chance, Kurapika reveals he is the sole survivor of the Kurta Clan after a group of bandits massacred them. This prompts the Captain to believe Kurapika wants to become a Blacklist Hunter, who confirms it and proclaims he doesn't fear death. Leorio then chimes in, stating his motive is to acquire money, prompting Kurapika to retort. Upset by Kurapika's remark, Leorio challenges Kurapika to a duel on the ship's deck, where the storm rages on. Amid this, Gon rescues a crew member who fell overboard and is sequentially rescued by Kurapika and Leorio. The courageous act convinces the Captain to pass all three of them to take the Hunter Exam.

Reaching the Exam Site, Gon, Leorio, and Kurapika are introduced to Tonpa, who offers them some drinks. To himself, Tonpa notes that the drinks he offered them were potent laxatives, but Gon vomits his up and returns his, stating they've expired. Taking them back, Tonpa ponders what went wrong until an examinee loses both arms to Examinee #44, Hisoka. Tonpa informs Gon and the group about Hisoka's backstory. The examiner for that phase, Satotz, then announces that the Hunter Exam is about to begin and that the phase's theme is a marathon run to the 2nd-phase site.

On route, Gon befriends a young boy his age named Killua, and he asks Tonpa for more juice, much to Tonpa's chagrin. As Satotz picks up the pace, Leorio rushes forward to stay ahead, and examinee #187 drops out due to exhaustion and the taunting by the Amori Brothers. The taunting was paid for by Tonpa, who thanked the bros. for what they did. Kurapika and Leorio also have a heart-to-heart about their respective reasons for wanting to become Hunters. Gon and Killua also have their own heart-to-heart about why they want to become Hunters.

At the tunnel exit, Satotz tells the examinees they're at the Milsy Wetlands and warns them about it. As the examinees trek through it, Hypnosis Butterflies, Ruse Ravens, and Claymore Mushrooms surround the area. Despite Killua's admonishment, Gon runs to where Leorio's cries originated.

Hisoka accosted Leorio, Kurpika, Cherry, Hanzo, and two examinees, informing them that he'd be playing the role of an examiner and would judge them on whether they were worthy of becoming Hunters. Cherry and the two examinees attacked Hisoka but died because of their efforts. With Kurapika, Leorio, and Hanzo remaining, Hanzo suggests they flee, noting they don't stand a chance against Hisoka. They do, and Hisoka allows them to flee for only 10 seconds. Leorio returns with a stick and intends to fight Hisoka, but is outclassed by him; however, Gon attacks Hisoka with his fishing rod, allowing Leorio to be spared, but only after Hisoka knocks him out. Hisoka takes an immediate interest in Gon and spares his life, too, and after getting contacted by someone, he carries Leorio out of the area.

Despondent after his encounter with Hisoka, Gon reunites with Kurapika and, following the scent of Leorio's collagen, reaches the second-phase exam site. Gon reunites with Killua and learns from Hisoka that Leorio was there.

Once Satotz takes his leave, Menchi and Buhara reveal and introduce themselves as Gourmet Hunters. Menchi announced that the 2nd-phase test is cooking, and they'll all have to prepare sushi for her. Menchi gives hints about what sushi is and how to prepare it for the clueless examinees. All but Hanzo knew what sushi was and how to prepare it, a delicacy in his home country.

When the examinees present their version of sushi to Menchi, she rejects them all. However, Hanzo confidently presents his sushi to Menchi, only to fail as well. Following Hanzo's lead, the remaining examinees prepare their own sushi, but also fail. Ultimately, Menchi fails them all, and one examinee, Todo, vents his frustrations over Menchi's examination process and tries to attack her, only to be knocked out by Buhara.

After Menchi's diatribe about the endeavors of being a Gourmet Hunter, the Chairman of the Exam, Netero, arrives, citing that failing all of the examinees was troublesome. Giving her reasons for her actions to Netero, Menchi decides to hold her test again and have the examinees fetch a Spider Eagle egg from Split Mountain and boil it. All but Todo manages to do this.

Afterward, Netero formally introduces himself to everyone, and Satotz announces that the next exam phase will begin at 8:00 am the next day and that they may use their time however they please in the meantime.

Meanwhile, Gon and Killua have a heart-to-heart about Killua's parents and family until Netero interrupts them and challenges them to a game of snatch the ball. If they win, Netero would give each of them a Hunter License. Killua tries first but fails; Gon then tries, but can't grab it either, so they team up, but still can't manage to snatch the ball from Netero. Frustrated, Killua admits defeat, but Gon wants to continue, knowing full well the futility of capturing the ball, but wants to get Netero to use his right hand, and he manages to succeed.

Upon arriving at the third exam site, the Trick Tower, Menchi and Satotz explained that they had to find a way into the tower and reach the bottom within 72 hours. Gon finds a way inside that lets five people through, and Kurapika, Leorio, and Killua follow Gon to the place, while Tonpa secretly follows them.

The 3rd phase examiner, Lippo, appears and orders his prisoners to welcome their guests. Once Gon and the group land on solid ground, they discover a sign that their trial will be decided by a majority vote. Soon after reading this, Tonpa arrives, and the group continues forward into the deeper parts of the tower, where they're confronted by Bendot, whose challenge is a deathmatch. Tonpa takes the challenge, only to surrender instantly. Tonpa then reveals his true nature and motives, which Leorio confronts him over, but Kurapika stops him from doing any harm to him.

Next, Gon faced off against Sedokan, whose match involved candles: the person whose candle went out first lost. Ultimately, Gon blows out Sedkan's candle before his candle runs out. Up next was Majtani, whom Kurapika faced off against, and although Majtani proposed a deathmatch too, Majtani was knocked out by Kurapika after he revealed his fake Phantom Troupe tattoo. Leorio was next and wanted to wager whether or not Majtani was alive, and Leroute appeared and wagered 10 hours on whether or not Majtani was alive. Leorio accepts and deduces that Majtani is still alive, but loses against Leroute in a game of rock-paper-scissors.

Finally, Killua faces off against Johness, who proposes that he simply kill Killua to satiate his desire to touch flesh again, but gets his heart ripped out by the boy instead.

Although Killua won his match against Johness, they're forced to remain in a special room for 50 hours because of Leorio's loss to Leroute. Reaching a fork in the road blocked by two doors, the group must choose between leaving behind two players or taking a route that'd take far longer to clear than their remaining time. Hence, Gon suggests they take the longer route and use the weapons there to clear a hole leading into the sacrificial route.

On the bottom floor of the tower, the examinees who've made it down there wait as Pokkle and Agon arrive. Soon, Gon's group arrives, too. Once they do, Lippo appears, wheeling a box on a tray, and announces the location of the next phase of the exam: Zevil Island. He then asks the examinees to draw a number from the box and explains the 4th phase of the exam.

On Zevil Island, Pokkle hunting Siper inspires Gon about how to capture Hisoka's badge. Meanwhile, Hisoka faces off against a mortally wounded Goz, whom Hisoka refuses to fight. As Goz struggles to land a blow against Hisoka, Hemotropic Butterflies surround him. Before Goz could attempt one more time to attack Hisoka, Gittarackur appears and, after pulling out a needle from his head, transforms. Gittarackur takes Goz's badge, gives the spare one he had to Hisoka, and takes his leave to rest. Now needing two more badges to pass the exam, Hisoka decides to go on a hunt.

Eventually, Gon trains enough to catch a Fisher Bird with his fishing rod, emulating the act of trying to catch a badge, and successfully does so. In doing so, his hands develop blood blisters and attract Hemotropic Butterflies to him, and Gon uses them to locate Hisoka.

Elsewhere, Leorio encounters Tonpa, who is suffering from an upset stomach. In exchange for the person whose badge Leorio was after, Leorio gives Tonpa some anti-diarrhea medication. However, this was a trap set up by Tonpa, who was working with another examinee named Sommy, who stole Leorio's badge from his briefcase. Before Tonpa and Sommy could escape, Kurapika arrived and incapacitated the two. Kurpika allows them to escape so long as they return Leorio's badge, and Sommy and Tonpa leave their badges behind as Tonpa is his target.

At that moment, Hisoka arrives and wants to take Leorio and Kurapika's badges; however, Kurapika reasons with Hisoka to let them go as they leave behind Tonpa's badge. Gon witnessed this entire encounter and wanted to use the opportunity to steal Hisoka's badge, but couldn't. He was also left terrified by Hisoka's presence, but follows him until he sees Agon and attacks him to steal his badge. Taking the opportunity, Gon steals Hisoka's badge as he kills Agon, and Gon flees.

By the end of the 4th phase, Hisoka, Pokkle, Gittarackur, Hanzo, Bodoro, Leorio, Gon, Kurapika, and Killua pass the exam. For reference for the final exam, Netero interviews Hisoka on who caught his eye the most and who he did not want to fight.

Later, Kurapika has a heart-to-heart with Gon about how the examinee Geretta incapacitated him, took his badge, and was then killed by Hisoka, who was his target. Although Hisoka returned Gon's badge to him, Gon wanted to return the badge he had stolen from Hisoka, but he refused it. He knocks out Gon and sets the condition that if Gon ever wants him to return his badge, he'll hit him in the face like he just did.

Netero explains the theme and rules for the final phase of the exam, then begins the first match, with Hanzo facing off against Gon. Gon wins because he refuses to surrender and does not hold a grudge against him after torturing him.

Afterward, Satotz congratulates Gon for becoming a Hunter, gives him his Hunter License, and then informs Gon on the order of who won their respective matches. Gon was most shocked about Killua's, as Gittarackur was really his older brother, Illumi, who forced him to surrender his match against him. Illumi also allegedly forced Killua to be disqualified from the exam after killing Bodoro. Upon learning this, Gon confronts Illumi and demands that he apologize to Killua, but he refuses, stating he is within his rights as Killua's older brother. Not accepting that, Gon grabs Illumi by his arm and pulls him away, and continues to argue with Illumi, who reveals that Killua had likely returned home to Kukuroo Mountain.

Hence, Gon, Leorio, and Kurapika set off to Kukuroo Mountain to locate Killua, who was chained to a wall and whipped by his older brother Milluki. This continues until Milluki receives a call from Kikyo, who details that Killua's friends have arrived.

Outside, Gon and the group reach the Testing Gate, where they're greeted by Zebro, who then explains the testing gate's function. So, Zebro contacts the butler liaison Gotoh to allow Gon and the group entry into the estate, but is refused. Therefore, Gon tries to talk to him, citing that he's Gon's friend, but Gotoh coldly states that he doesn't have friends and hangs up on him. Gon redials and snaps at Gotoh, demanding that Killua be released, but Gotoh refuses, citing the risks involved. Seeing the determination of Gon and his friends, Zebro allows them to take an alternate route and train so they can open the first set of doors at the testing gate.

Back inside the estate, Milluki continues whipping Killua until Zeno arrives, and he tells Killua that Silva wants to see him. After Killua leaves, Milluki shares his opinion of Killua with Zeno, then tells him about his assassination device involving a mosquito, which Zeno isn't impressed with.

On Gon's side, the group makes it through the Testing Gate and is confronted by butler-in-training Canary, who refuses to allow the group to progress. However, Gon insists on doing so and is beaten by Canary until she submits and requests that they help Killua, but Kikyo and Kalluto intervene. Kikyo tells the group that Killua is locked away in a cell of his own volition but panics after learning Zeno has released him. The sudden revelation makes Kikyo and Kalluto leave, but not before Gon asks Kikyo to relay a message to Killua for him.

After Canary awakens, she promises to take them to Killua, who is having a heart-to-heart with his father, Silva, who asks him if he wants to see his friends. Killua answers yes, and Silva allows him to go under the condition that he doesn't betray his friends. As Killua leaves, Kikyo tries to stop him, but Killua demands she let him go.

Meanwhile, Gon and the group reach the butler's quarters, where Gotoh and the other butlers greet them. Gotoh tells them that Killua is on his way and suggests they play a coin game to pass the time. However, the game becomes progressively difficult as Gotoh reveals his resentment toward the group and holds them hostage by knifepoint. Both Leorio and Kurapika are knocked out after guessing wrong in each round, but Gon wins by guessing the final one correctly.

Killua then arrives, and then all, but Gon leave and play one last coin game with Gotoh and surprisingly lose it.

As Kikyo laments that Silva allowed Killua to leave after he returned, Silva says he'll return one day because he's his son.

Outside of the estate, Gon, Killua, Kurapika, and Leorio all go their separate ways, but plan to reunite in Yorknew City on September 1st.

Part 2

Kurapika laments over the loss of his Kurta Clan brethren and curses the culprits who killed them.

Mito hangs some laundry on Whale Island until Gon and Killua arrive. After testing whether or not the Hunter License could break or not, the two tell Mito about their time in Heavens Arena.

Later, Gon and Killua have a heart-to-heart conversation about their intentions and their parents. Mito overhears this part of how Gon considers Mito his mother.

Kurapika enters the Sengi Guild hoping to get a job; however, the Sengi Guild Agent refuses, claiming he cannot see and details his exam isn't over yet.

Mito appears, briefly explains her relationship with Ging to Gon, and hands him the box that Ging had entrusted to her.

Later, Gon and Killua attempt to open it using Nen, a technique their teacher, Wing, taught them.

A flashback shows Gon and Killua reaching the 200th floor of Heaven's Arena, where they're confronted by Hisoka, who excuses his Hatsu, which prevents the two from progressing further. Wing appears and assists the two in escaping, explaining to them the fundamental facts about being baptized in Nen. He then baptizes them and teaches them how to use Ten, which allows the two to bypass Hisoka's Hatsu. Gon tries to return Hisoka's number plate, but Hisoka refuses, citing the condition he placed for its return on Gon.

Training his newfound Nen powers, Kurapika is told by his master Izunavi that he's made impressive strides in his four months of Nen training. Izunavi then suggests developing a Nen ability based on the Nen type he falls under. Simultaneously, Wing explains the six Nen types to Gon and Killua. From their Water Divinations, Kurapika is a Conjurer, Gon is an Enhancer, and Killua is a Transmuter. After four weeks of training, Gon and Killua display their efforts to Wing, who passes them both and tells Gon that he passed the Secret Hunter Exam.

Gon then faces off against Hisoka, and although he manages to hit Hisoka in the face, allowing him to return Hisoka's badge, Gon still loses to Hisoka due to a technicality by the referee.

Ringing the doorbell to the Nostrade's Mansion, Kurapika is met by the Nostrade Butler, who welcomes him to the estate and allows him to enter.

A flashback shows Kurapika meeting the Sengi Guild Agent again, and she notes how impressed she was by how quickly Kurapika mastered Nen. Kurapika requests a job with someone with connections to the Yorknew City Underground Auction, and she suggests the Flesh collector.

At present, the butler shows potential job applicants a video by a man who instructs them to find an item listed on a card to pass. However, he also says they must first escape the mansion, only for unknown assailants to ambush the group, until Kurapika locates the man controlling them all. Held by knifepoint by Kurapika, the man calls off the assailants and introduces himself as Shachmono Tocino, one whom the mansion's owner hired. Tocino then says he hopes the four of them can escape the mansion alive, alluding to another plant amongst them, so Kurapika uses his Dowsing Chain to locate the last plant, and it was a man named Squala. Who cracks under the pressure from Kurapika, Melody, and Basho confessing that he's the plant.

Another flashback depicts Izunavi asking Kurapika what kind of Nen ability he wants to create, and he responds, "Chains." Once that is settled, the applicants leave the mansion.

After taking a shower, Hisoka is visited by Machi, who delivers a message from the Phantom Troupe leader to meet in Yorknew City by August 30th. Although Hisoka offers Machi to enjoy dinner together, she doesn't respond to his offer, and Hisoka's spider tattoo is revealed, only for him to reveal it is fake.

On Whale Island, Gon and Killua listen to the tape left by Ging, but stop it when Ging talks about Gon's mother. This causes the tape to overwrite the recording, which Gon and Killua futilely try to prevent.

Meanwhile, Kurapika and the other bodyguard applicants presented the items specified on the list to Dalzollene and passed the test he had set. Dalzollene congratulates them and brings them to their boss, Neon Nostrade. The new bodyguards talk about their new boss, and Basho claims her father managed to gain power in the Mafia Community. Still, Kurapika corrects him, stating he used his daughter's power to accomplish that.

In Yorknew, the Phantom Troupe gathers and learns from their leader, Chrollo, that they'll be stealing all of the items up for auction at the Underground Auction.

On September 1st, Gon and Killua arrive in Yorknew and are both fascinated by the merchants and their wares for sale. They take particular interest in a merchant selling cell phones, but Leorio arrives and shows off his own cellphone, the Beatle-07. Interested in purchasing two Beatle-07s for Gon and Killua, Leorio successfully haggles with the cellphone merchant down to a more reasonable price. Learning that the two need money to purchase a game at the auction to be able to meet Gon's Dad, Leorio hatches a plan to raise enough money to accomplish this.

Neon demonstrates her Lovely Ghost Writer ability, and Dalzollene reads the ominous descriptions that the predictions foretell. Dalzollene relays this to his boss, Light Nostrade, who forbids Neon from attending the upcoming Underground Auction, despite protests from Dalzollene.

Dalzollene informs the bodyguards of their respective tasks for the Underground Auction. Neon then appears, and Dalzollene is forced to inform Neon that she's forbidden to attend the auction under her father's orders. Dalzollene and Squala take away Neon.

Elsewhere, Gon arm wrestles with random people on the streets of Yorknew City for 10k a piece. Of all the people Gon wrestles, a woman gives him the most difficulty. The woman's name was Shizuku, a member of the Phantom Troupe, and she later regroups with members, Feitan and Franklin.

On the rooftop, they were stationed to oversee, Melody and Kurapika have a heart-to-heart about why they joined the Mafia Community. Kurapika reveals that he wants to retrieve his brethren's stolen Scarlet Eyes and Melody to locate and destroy the Sonata of Darkness. The conversation ends when they hear gunshots originating from the auction site.

Mafia members scramble and fall over after being hit by bullets. Tocino and Baise identify the bullets as Nen bullets and dodge long enough to come up with a simple plan, for Tocino to hold them off and Baise to find a way to escape and inform the others of the emergency. Tocino activates his Eleven Black Children, but shortly after they are summoned, they are destroyed by Franklin's Double Machine Gun, and Tocino collapses. Baise makes it a short distance before Shizuku appears in front of her, and is swiftly killed by Shizuku striking her in the back of her head with Blinky as she turns to run.

After the massacre, Franklin complains about how anticlimactic it was, and Feitan grouses about not having a chance to do anything. Franklin tells Shizuku to clean up the body parts, and the mess is vacuumed up by Blinky. They notice that Tocino is still alive because he hasn't been sucked up, and as they approach him, he curses them, saying his friends will definitely get them. Franklin kills Tocino, and Blinky vacuums up his corpse.

A large commotion forms as the mafia discovers the empty auction house. Kurapika and Melody overhear the discussion, and Kurapika is enraged to hear that the Spiders are involved. Elsewhere, Uvogin calls Chrollo to let him know the treasure wasn't there, as if the mafia had known about the attack in advance. Dalzollene and Squala contact Kurapika and Melody and plan to meet at the overpass near the city's central plaza to catch the Spiders and retrieve their stolen goods. Uvogin tells Chrollo that he believes that there is a traitor among them, but Chrollo logically dismantles his argument and suggests that the Ten Dons knew about it. Uvogin reports that one man had entered the auction's safe with nothing, but the safe had been emptied by the time he left. Sensing the use of Nen abilities among their opponents, Chrollo tells Uvogin to go on a rampage and handle them. Uvogin hangs up and laughs in excitement.

While at the hideout, Hisoka gets up to leave. Pakunoda asks him where he's going, and Hisoka tells them that he has an appointment he forgot about. He asks Chrollo if it's all right to leave, promising to return by 6 p.m. the next day. He gets permission and leaves.

The mafia members catch up to the Spiders' hot air balloon as it lands, firing gunshots and making loud noises in the process. Uvogin steps up to confront them, and one of the mafia members accuses them of being the ones who killed their companions. Uvogin laughs and admits that they were, and asks the mafia member what the matter was. Without responding, the mafia member shoots Uvogin in the face, and they are horrified to see him catch the bullet with his teeth. Uvogin tears apart the mafia members, even handling a sniper and a rocket launcher.

As a rocket explodes in Uvogin's face, Neon's bodyguards arrive a distance away and try to figure out what is going on. Suddenly, while Uvogin's back is to them, Kurapika spots the tattoo of a spider on his back and begins to walk slowly towards him without saying a word to his companions. They tell him there is no way he can deal with their strength, but Kurapika continues, even ignoring Dalzollene's orders to stop, until Melody plays Nono's song to help the group calm down. Kurapika tells his leader that he has a plan and calmly leaves. 

After the fight is over, the spiders start to relax and figure out what to do next, but Uvogin is suddenly wrapped in chains and yanked away. Machi thinks quickly and throws a needle at Uvogin to follow him, and the Spiders decide to follow Uvogin.

When Uvogin next wakes up, he finds himself restrained with chains on a table. Dalzollene asks where the auction's stolen goods are, but Uvogin tells him that he must have imagined things. Uvogin says that the poison that they gave him isn't working. Dalzollene tells Uvogin to listen to him and asks whether Uvogin knows the position he is in. However, Uvogin offers Dalzollene a deal: let him go, and he will spare their lives, saying that the Spiders only wanted the goods from the auction. Melody confirms that he was not the one who took it, and Uvogin reports that the mafia community had taken it away before the items could be stolen. Kurapika asks what happened to the attendees, and Uvogin tells them that they were all killed. Dalzollene tells the group to confirm with the higher-ups what had happened as he watches over Uvogin with Squala. Kurapika receives a notification on his phone and takes a different route.

Kurapika meets up with Hisoka and tells him that he does not have time to relax. Hisoka tells him that there were 13 Spiders, identifiable by their tattoo, and that he joined as Member #4 to get the chance to fight Chrollo. Hisoka offers information in exchange for Kurapika's help in securing a fight with Chrollo. Kurapika asks if Hisoka knows about the eyes, but Hisoka says that it was before he joined the group, and the items had likely already been sold off. Hisoka tells Kurapika that as long as the head is not crushed, the Spiders will keep moving. Right before Kurapika can respond to Hisoka's offer, he gets a call from Melody, letting him know that Spider #11 had escaped and that they could not contact Dalzollene. Kurapika tells Hisoka they will meet at the same time the next day and leaves.

Neon laments to her bodyguards that the auction was canceled and she didn't get her mummy from it. However, Melody assures her that the auction items have been moved, which cheers her up. Kurapika informs Neon of the deaths of Tocino and Baise. Dalzollene. Furthermore, he wishes to speak to her father, the newly elected leader, so Neon contacts Light, who speaks to him. When Kurapika does, he insists that Neon be escorted out of Yorknew to ensure her safety, to which Light agrees, stating he'll arrive the next day. While Neon is taken to her room, Kurapika gives orders to Basho and Squala, as Gon and Killua unsuccessfully try to contact him. Leorio approaches the boys and informs them about the bounties on the Phantom Troupe's heads while Uvogin confronts Kurapika.

At the Gordeau Desert, Kurapika faces off against Uvogin in a deathmatch and successfully entraps Uvogin in his Chain Jail while using Emperor Time. Despite his efforts in his interrogations, Uvogin refuses to answer any of Kurapika's questions and is subsequently killed by Kurapika's Judgment Chain.

In their hideout, the Phantom Troupe grows concerned about Uvogin's absence. Chrollo suggests that if he doesn't return by dawn, their plans will change, and they'll hunt the "Chain User".

On September 3rd, Gon and Killua procure some merchandise they were imbued with Nen, and as they head to sell it to a merchant, a man follows them. The items Gon and Killua sell fetch a high price with the merchant; however, one particular item, a wooden statue, is said to be of paltry worth. The man then arrives and proclaims that the merchant is lying about the statue's value, detailing its history.

Once they leave the merchant's store, the man agrees to be paid a meal for his services. At the restaurant, the man introduces himself as Zepile. After explaining his background as a former counterfeit artist and learning about Gon and Killua's hope to acquire a game to find his father, Zepile decides to help the boys. He then explains to them about a counterfeiting technique and how the merchant planned to steal the treasure from the wooden statue. Receiving a call from Leorio with news about the Phantom Troupe, the boys leave Zepile to regroup with him and entrust Zepile with the treasure.

Meanwhile, Light instructs Basho and Melody to guide Neon back to the mansion. With all of them gone, Light tells Kurapika that the auction is back on. This is because the Mafia community hired Freelance Assassins, Silva, and Zeno Zoldyck.

Later, high-ranking mafioso Zenji instructs the assassins of their targets while Kurapika and Light listen in. Once the meeting was over, Light got into a spat with Zenji, and Kurapika intervened with a knife.

Nobunaga and Machi discuss the possibility that Uvogin is dead. When Nobunaga spotted them, Gon and Killua tried to make a run for it, but Pakunoda and Phinks stopped them. The two are taken back to the Phantom Troupe's hideout, and Gon immediately recognizes Hisoka, but retracts it, and Killua notices Shizuku, whom Gon once arm-wrestled to counter this. Interested in arm wrestling Gon himself, Nobunaga challenges Gon in some matches and wins nearly all of them while he talks about Uvogin. Enraged, Gon calls Nobunaga a hypocrite and wins a match, causing Feitan to restrain him and Hisoka to hold Killua at his mercy. Nobunaga settles the situation in a coin toss: the boys are freed, and he asks Gon to join the troupe, but Gon refuses. Still, Nobunaga insists and takes the boys to a different room. Shalnark then passes each member a paper with pictures of all the current Nostrade Family bodyguards. The Phantom Troupe members then split up into groups of two and, to Machi's dismay, are paired with Hisoka.

Seated in the back of a car with Light, Kurapika is contacted by Melody, who informs him that Neon has escaped. Light contacts the local law enforcers to watch for anyone who matches Neon's description. Neon, who was at the Cemetery Building, wrote a prediction for Chrollo that confirmed Uvogin's death. Chrollo, who cried after reading the prediction, knocks Neon out and uses her background as a Nostrade Family member to manipulate the Mafiosi there to call an ambulance for her. Assassin A watches video feedback of Chrollo attacking Neon and tries to hunt him down, only to be killed by Chrollo's Indoor Fish. Chrollo then gives the signal to his members to massacre the mafia.

Finally reaching Kurapika, Gon talks to him, but a flashback shows how Gon and Killua escaped from Nobunaga first. Kurapika lashes out at the two for their recklessness. Killua, though, deduces that Kurapika is the Chain User that the Phantom Troupe is after.

Elsewhere, Zeno and Silva face off against Chrollo, but stop after Illumi reports that his assassination of the Ten Dons was successful. From Zenji and his guards, Kurapika learns that the leader of the Phantom Troupe is dead and is ordered by Light to retrieve the Scarlet Eyes up for auction. With the Scarlet Eyes in his possession, Kurapika intimidates Zenji, who tries to steal them from him.

Inside the Phantom Troupe's hideout, Nobunaga disagrees with Chrollo about how to proceed. Hence, Chrollo decides to use his newly stolen ability to read Nobunaga's fortune. It predicts that five members will likely die within the next week, and after reading Nobunaga's, Shizuku deduces that she, Pakunoda, and Shalnark will be among the deceased. All but Kortopi, Feitan, and Phinks get their fortunes read due to a lack of knowledge of what's required for the prediction.

Gon, Kurapika, Leorio, and Killua all reunite, and Kurapika discloses his Nen ability to them, which Killua lashes out at Him for because one member can read memories. Although Kurapika wanted to focus on retrieving the eyes of his brethren, a text from Hisoka reveals that the bodies found in the Phantom Troupe were fake. He then gets contacted by Melody, who informs him that the Ten Dons have canceled their bounty on the Phantom Troupe as they're from Meteor City. Earnest in his plea to help Kurapika in his endeavor to hunt the Phantom Troupe, Gon listens to Kurapika's further explanation of his Nen abilities. Despite Gon wanting a Nen blade pointed at his heart, as did Killua and Leorio, Kurapika refused to do so.

After reading his fortune, Pakunoda asks Hisoka to read it, and reluctantly, Hisoka agrees. Pakunoda's reading of it reveals Hisoka's treachery, and Nobunaga becomes furious but is stopped by Franklin. Shalnark questions Hisoka, who refuses to answer his questions. When Chrollo does, he answers a couple but rejects the ones that reveal or insinuate the Chain User's identity. This leads Chrollo to decide to stay in Yorknew and deduces that the Chain User is likely a survivor of the Kurta Clan. The Phantom Troupe breaks up into groups by tracking the location of the fake Scarlet Eyes by using Kortopi's En.

Perched on a building, Killua teams up with Melody, who assists him in tracking the Phantom Troupe's movement. Killua relays his findings to Kurapika, who sits in the backseat of a car with Gon as Leorio drives. Realizing the Phantom Troupe was heading to where Squala was, he contacts and warns him.

Eventually, Gon and Killua are captured by Chrollo, Shizuku, and Machi and are taken to the Hotel Beitacle. Squala is interrogated by Pakunoda, who, after he snaps at her, is killed by Nobunaga. Pakunoda then uses her Memory Bomb on Nobunaga and Kortopi to reveal Kurapika's identity to them.

At the hotel, Leorio surreptitiously relays a message to Gon and Killua about a blackout scheduled for 7:00 p.m. Soon, Pakunoda's group arrives, and Chrollo asks her to ask them what they're hiding. She does, and the blackout occurs when she learns what the boys are hiding. Leorio and Chrollo vanish, leaving a message from Kurapika behind, telling his comrades not to reveal his abilities. As Pakunoda contemplates her situation, Nobunaga tells her not to speak, or Chrollo's life will be jeopardized.

Driving alongside Chrollo, who was wrapped in his chains, Kurapika tried to interrogate him, but Chrollo's confidence outmaneuvered him.

Phinks, Feitan, and Shalnark arrive at the hotel and are updated on the situation. Kurapika then contacts them and gives his demands to Phinks, demanding to speak to Pakunoda after setting up a meeting point with her at Lingon Airport. Pakunoda listens to Kurapika's demands for the hostage exchange between Chrollo, Gon, and Killua. She agrees to all of Kurapika's terms imposed on her by him.

Returning to the base, Pakunoda quarrels with Phinks, who then argues with Gon and reasons with Franklin to let Pakunoda leave with the boys.

Once the hostage exchange goes smoothly, Hisoka appears and challenges Chrollo to a deathmatch. He is astonished to learn that Chrollo can no longer use his Nen.

Returning to the base, Pakunoda uses her Memory Bomb on Machi, Phinks, Feitan, Nobunaga, and Franklin to relay her thoughts, feelings, and critical information on Kurapika.

Melody stays by Kurapika's side as he lies and recovers, and Leorio says his goodbyes to continue studying for his medical exams.

Meanwhile, Gon and Killua run into Phinks and Feitan at the auction, who note they have no intention of harming them as Chrollo still has a Nen dagger aimed at his heart. Phinks also confirms that Pakunoda has died, but before leaving, relays that she is grateful toward them.

Kurapika awakens and expresses his gratitude for his friends.

Part 3

The play includes many parts, of which the most prominent are:

Entry to Greed Island

Gon enters Greed Island, where he is welcomed by Eta. Once inside the game, Gon and Killua are suddenly confronted by Latarza, who flies toward them and attacks Killua with a game spell. Later, Gon, Killua, Nickes, and Asta are relaxing in Antokiba when their conversation is interrupted by a loud scream. Rushing to the source, they discover a new disturbance. At Antokiba’s plaza, Gon, Killua, Genthru, Puhat, Bisky, and others gather in discussion. Genthru brings up the previous incident: that man who died earlier was killed by the Bomber. When Puhat asks how cards can be obtained, one player explains that they can be found or traded, while Killua adds that they can also be stolen. When asked to join their alliance, Gon refuses while Killua follows.

Later, in another part of the island, Gon and Killua are cornered by three Cyclops. Instead of fear, they show excitement at the challenge and fight them in a slow, choreographed battle. Emerging victorious, they receive two Cyclone cards. Meanwhile, Bisky watches the boys and laments how they are wasting their potential. An armored opponent soon appears behind them, and after Bisky shouts at them to use Gyo, she declares it is time to train them. She mentions that their training will be rough, and when she reveals her true age, Killua calls her old, prompting Bisky to punch him.

The Bomber

Back at the Nicks' alliance's hideout, Genthru reveals himself as the Bomber and announces that bombs have already been placed on everyone. When Jispa attempts to retaliate, Genthru uses Little Flower, blowing his face up and making it clear how dangerous he truly is. After leaving the hideout, Genthru, Sub, and Bara meet a few members from Nickes' Alliance at Greed Island’s entry point. In return for giving away their monopolized cards, Genthru cancels out his bombs as he collides his thumb with his partners' and shouts “Release!”. Instead of disappearing, the bombs detonate in a staged fireworks-and-smoke display. Afterwards, Genthru cruelly kicks Nickes around, calling him scum and laughing maniacally at how easily they were deceived.

Vs. Razor

Gon and Killua battle Binolt, using giant rocks to guard against his scissors while Bisky observes from above. This transitions into a musical scene where Bisky sings, describing Gon as a Diamond and Killua as a Sapphire, highlighting their qualities while the boys dance around the stage and interact with props. Thanks to Bisky's training, when Killua left the game, he easily cleared the hunter exam and earned his license. 

Razor and some of his lackeys appear for their own musical sequence, while Kortopi, Shizuku, Shalnark, Feitan, and Phinks make their entrance to the island by boat. Afterward, Hisoka shares a moment with Nobunaga and Machi, asking what she would do if he defeated Chrollo. Her response was that it was impossible, but if it happened, she would hunt him down. When Gon, Killua, Bisky, and Goreinu came after the fake Chrollo, a comedic interlude followed with Hisoka’s bathing musical, where he appears in briefs as extras in leaf costumes scramble to cover him.

Next came the dodgeball match against Razor. Gon, Killua, Bisky, Hisoka, Goreinu, Tsezguerra, and his companions face Razor and his Nen beasts. Goreinu summons his two gorillas to make up for the two missing members necessary to begin the match. As the match ensues, only Gon, Killua, and Hisoka are left to face Razor. This is when the three broke into song about their roles and their determination to catch Razor's ball as they formed up. On the other side of the stage, Razor sends the ball away while an extra carries it across the stage. The struggle intensifies until the music swells without lyrics: Gon calls on his strength, Hisoka uses Bungee Gum to catch and return the ball, and Razor is finally defeated.

Facing The Bomber

After the match, Tsezguerra receives a call from Genthru, while Gon listens carefully. In preparation for their inevitable battle against the bombers, the three contemplate how short the time they have. In another musical interlude, Bisky sings alongside Cookie-chan, giving the scene a softer tone. Soon after, the Bombers begin their hunt, and during their separate battles, Bisky briefly summons her full body's strength.

In the finale, all cast members return to the stage — Gon, Killua, Bisky, Genthru, Razor, Hisoka, the Phantom Troupe, and others — joining together in one last full musical. The chaos of battles, betrayals, and training culminates in a colorful ensemble, bringing the Greed Island play to a spectacular close.}}
